w20: Accelerator-optimized instance featuring a single NVIDIA A100 GPU with 40GB VRAM, suitable for demanding machine learning and high-performance computing workloads.
c150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g: Accelerator-optimized with NVIDIA A100 GPU, 12 vCPUs, 85GB RAM. Ideal for AI/ML, HPC, and data science.
c200: Google Cloud Platform's a2-highgpu-1g features a dedicated NVIDIA A100 GPU with 40GB VRAM, 12 x86_64 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 85GB RAM. Optimized for intensive AI training, scientific simulations, and complex data processing.
w150: Google Cloud Platform a2-highgpu-1g is an accelerator-optimized instance. It features an x86_64 Intel Xeon CPU with 12 virtual cores and 6 physical cores operating at 2.2 GHz, providing dedicated CPU allocation. The system includes 85 GB of memory. A single NVIDIA A100 GPU with 40 GB of VRAM is bundled, providing substantial parallel processing capabilities. Local storage is not included. Benchmark results indicate top-tier LLM inference speeds for prompt processing (8237.32 tokens/sec for 135M model) and text generation (339.23 tokens/sec for 135M model), also demonstrating strong performance with medium (7B) models. Conversely, single-core CPU performance is weak, and multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and compression tasks are categorized as average. This instance is primarily designed for demanding AI/ML training, deep learning inference, scientific computing, and data science jobs that heavily leverage GPU acceleration, accepting the trade-off of average general-purpose CPU capabilities.
